description: Rate limiting in ai-orchestrator — ai_rate_limits table + sliding window + 429 response

migration.evidence: Migration 20260310000001_create_ai_rate_limits.sql creates ai_rate_limits table (user_id PK, request_count, window_start), RLS enabled with 2 policies (users read own, service_role manages all), check_rate_limit() SECURITY DEFINER RPC with sliding 60-second window, row-level locking via FOR UPDATE.

edge_function.evidence: ai-orchestrator step 4 (lines 370-404): looks up user subscription_tier from user_profiles, maps to rate limit (starter=5, pro=15, enterprise=60), calls check_rate_limit RPC, returns 429 with code 'rate_limit_exceeded' when exceeded. Fails open on infrastructure errors.

rls.evidence: RLS enabled on ai_rate_limits. Policy 'Users can read own rate limit' (SELECT, auth.uid()=user_id). Policy 'Service role manages rate limits' (ALL, auth.role()='service_role').

tier_config.evidence: Rate limits match owner decision #7: starter=5/min, pro=15/min, enterprise=60/min. Default fallback to starter tier.
